You will be given the current Python program with its score and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Artist. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Predicates that describe a relationship where the object is the primary entity
    relational_predicates = {
        "leader", "capital", "currency", "anthem", "parentCompany", "location",
        "governingBody", "leaderTitle", "country", "isPartOf", "derivative",
        "recordLabel", "bandMember", "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist"
    }

    def format_triple(t: Triple) -> str:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
        if p in relational_predicates:
            # e.g., "Elizabeth II is the leader of United Kingdom"
            return f"{o} is the {p} of {s}"
        if p in ("birthDate", "birthYear"):
            return f"{s} was born in {o}"
        if p in ("deathDate", "deathPlace"):
            return f"{s} died in {o}"
        if p == "birthPlace":
            return f"{s} was born in {o}"
        if p == "origin":
            return f"{s} originates from {o}"
        if p == "genre":
            return f"{s} performs {o} music"
        if p == "instrument":
            return f"{s} plays the {o}"
        if p == "occupation":
            return f"{s} works as {o}"
        if p == "nationality":
            return f"{s} has nationality {o}"
        if p == "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist":
            return f"{s} is associated with {o}"
        if p == "recordLabel":
            return f"{s} is signed to {o}"
        if p == "background":
            return f"{s} has a background as {o}"
        if p == "activeYearsStartYear":
            return f"{s} started activity in {o}"
        if p == "activeYearsEndYear":
            return f"{s} ended activity in {o}"
        # default fallback
        return f"{s} {p} {o}"

    # Combine objects that share the same subject and predicate
    from collections import defaultdict

    grouped: dict[tuple[str, str], list[str]] = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        grouped[(t.subject, t.predicate)].append(t.object)

    combined_triples: list[Triple] = []
    for (s, p), objs in grouped.items():
        if len(objs) == 1:
            o = objs[0]
        else:
            # join multiple objects with commas and "and"
            o = ", ".join(objs[:-1]) + " and " + objs[-1]
        combined_triples.append(Triple(s, p, o))

    # Generate a clause for each (possibly combined) triple
    clauses = [format_triple(t) for t in combined_triples]

    # Combine clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to lower‑case genre/style names for natural phrasing
    def lc(text: str) -> str:
        return text.lower()

    # Index triples by subject for quick lookup
    from collections import defaultdict, OrderedDict

    subj_map: dict[str, list[Triple]] = defaultdict(list)
    order: list[str] = []  # preserve first‑seen subject order
    for t in triples:
        subj_map[t.subject].append(t)
        if t.subject not in order:
            order.append(t.subject)

    sentences: list[str] = []

    for subj in order:
        parts: list[str] = []
        triples_here = subj_map[subj]

        # ----- genre handling (with possible stylisticOrigin of the genre) -----
        genre_triples = [t for t in triples_here if t.predicate == "genre"]
        if genre_triples:
            genre_obj = genre_triples[0].object
            phrase = f"{subj} plays {lc(genre_obj)} music"
            # look for a triple where the genre is the subject and has stylisticOrigin
            for t in triples:
                if t.subject == genre_obj and t.predicate == "stylisticOrigin":
                    phrase += f", which originated from {lc(t.object)}"
                    break
            parts.append(phrase)

        # ----- associated band / musical artist -----
        assoc = [t for t in triples_here if t.predicate == "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist"]
        if assoc:
            band = assoc[0].object
            parts.append(f"{subj} played with the band {band}")

        # ----- basic personal info -----
        for t in triples_here:
            s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
            if p in ("birthDate", "birthYear"):
                parts.append(f"{subj} was born in {o}")
            elif p in ("deathDate", "deathPlace"):
                parts.append(f"{subj} died in {o}")
            elif p == "birthPlace":
                parts.append(f"{subj} was born in {o}")
            elif p == "origin":
                parts.append(f"{subj} originates from {o}")
            elif p == "instrument":
                parts.append(f"{subj} plays the {o}")
            elif p == "occupation":
                parts.append(f"{subj} works as {o}")
            elif p == "nationality":
                parts.append(f"{subj} is {o}")
            elif p == "recordLabel":
                parts.append(f"{subj} is signed to {o}")
            elif p == "background":
                parts.append(f"{subj} has a background as {o}")
            elif p == "activeYearsStartYear":
                parts.append(f"{subj} started activity in {o}")
            elif p == "activeYearsEndYear":
                parts.append(f"{subj} ended activity in {o}")

        # ----- relational predicates where object is primary -----
        relational_predicates = {
            "leader", "capital", "currency", "anthem", "parentCompany", "location",
            "governingBody", "leaderTitle", "country", "isPartOf", "derivative",
            "bandMember"
        }
        for t in triples_here:
            if t.predicate in relational_predicates:
                parts.append(f"{t.object} is the {t.predicate} of {subj}")

        # Combine parts for this subject
        if parts:
            if len(parts) == 1:
                sentences.append(parts[0] + ".")
            else:
                sentences.append(", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1] + ".")

    # Join sentences for different subjects
    return " ".join(sentences)
